Container.Name: UmmonGlyphUI
Container.Overview: Ummon Glyph UI — A deterministic visual glyph system for Home Assistant. Procedurally generated grid-based colored block patterns that create a consistent, non-human symbolic visual language for smart home notifications and status display.
Container.Description: Ummon Glyph UI generates deterministic visual glyphs from Home Assistant events. Each glyph encodes domain, device, status, and urgency into a unique grid-based pattern. Features: admin dashboard, SSE real-time updates, PNG/MJPEG stream, seed-based theming, Home Assistant entity integration with label-based filtering.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID — use 99 for Unraid 'nobody' user.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID — use 100 for Unraid 'users' group.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the Ummon Glyph UI web interface.

Config.Name: Config Directory
Config.Description: Persistent config directory — stores credentials, style seed, domain/intent definitions, HA integration config.

Config.Name: Admin Username
Config.Description: Username for admin portal authentication. Leave empty to disable auth.

Config.Name: Admin Password
Config.Description: Password for admin portal authentication. Stored bcrypt-hashed.

Config.Name: API Key
Config.Description: API key for glyph ingestion endpoints (POST /glyph, POST /clear). Leave empty for open access.

Config.Name: Style Seed
Config.Description: Initial seed string for glyph theming. Can also be set via admin UI.
